subtitle: The Atlantic shore where Puerto Rico's tourism industry began, at the 1919 Condado Vanderbilt Hotel, and where the open-ocean rip currents now make it one of the island's deadliest beaches to swim.
spike_statement: Puerto Rico's tourism began at the 1919 Condado Vanderbilt Hotel; the open-Atlantic surf on the beach out front is now among the island's deadliest.

levers.photo_tone: Beaux-Arts white walls and red Spanish tile of the Condado Vanderbilt above an open Atlantic shorebreak, Ashford Avenue towers behind quartz-pale sand, a winter swell breaking hard on an exposed urban coast
